mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or median 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically greater than zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50% over a 24-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in private demand, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a negative mean return over 60 days in > 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening manufacturing output that reduces near-term corporate revenue growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month trailing average
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered cases during late-cycle expansions, or the predictive slope coefficient turns positive in a regime-filtered panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real personal consumption expenditures reflect stronger household purchasing power that directly boosts corporate sales expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 36-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following consumption increases are not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.0 percent imposes higher financing costs on leveraged firms and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where the rate threshold is breached, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in a forward-testing framework.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and declining total nonfarm payrolls signals accelerating labor market deterioration that forces equity investors to downgrade earnings models, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal fails to predict negative returns with > 52% accuracy over a 48-month out-of-sample period, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for macro volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent from the prior month indicates a sharp pullback in construction activity that reduces near-term capital goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances during housing corrections, or the predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.02 in a regime-specific backtest.
